description: 18 topics + Topic 1b organised into three layers (Foundation, Clinical, Context/Future). Baseline is 2 T1 seeds per topic, with targeted augmentation where citation-structure coverage is thin. Topics use TOPIC-## prefix to distinguish from corpus tier codes (T1–T4). v1.3 changes: (1) TOPIC-16 Research Priorities retired — Roadmap papers promoted to framing seeds R7/R8; (2) New TOPIC-16 Symptom Management & Multidisciplinary Care added with seeds MS-T1-047/MS-T1-048; (3) TOPIC-06 rebalanced (GFAP + McDonald 2024 added, Bittner NfL retired); (4) TOPIC-07 rebalanced (AFFIRM + EXPAND added, ORATORIO redistributed to TOPIC-08); (5) TOPIC-14 gains MS-T1-031 (AI reclassification); (6) TOPIC-15 renamed Remyelination & Neuroprotection, gains Lubetzki 2020; (7) TOPIC-13 gains Okai 2022 as fifth seed.

layers.Foundation: Topics TOPIC-00, TOPIC-01, TOPIC-1b, TOPIC-02–TOPIC-04 — biological vocabulary and disease framing. Read first.
layers.Clinical: Topics TOPIC-05–TOPIC-11 — diagnosis, treatment, management. Presuppose Foundation layer.
layers.Context/Future: Topics TOPIC-12–TOPIC-15 — equity, pediatric, AI, frontiers. Most meaningful after Clinical layer.

topics.why_it_matters: Quantitative global frame; geographic diversity; disease scale worldwide
RRMS/SPMS/PPMS/CIS/RIS taxonomy; diagnostic criteria; prerequisite vocabulary for all other topics
Pre-DMT disease trajectory; disability progression independent of relapses; baseline for treatment effect measurement
topics.monitor_overlap: Verify post-rerun that TOPIC-1b corpus is distinct from TOPIC-08 — Lyon cohort and T08 citation neighborhoods overlap. If majority overlap confirmed, consider merging.

topics.why_it_matters: Demyelination vs neurodegeneration duality; lesion biology; why peripheral immune drugs fail in progressive MS
HLA architecture; 200+ GWAS loci; polygenic risk; why MS is an immune-dysregulation disease
EBV; vitamin D; smoking; latitude; multi-factor model; emerging vs settled science
MRI criteria; gadolinium; PRL; central vein sign; evolving McDonald criteria
NfL; GFAP; CHI3L1; serum vs CSF; treatment monitoring; prognostic use; 2024 biomarker-integrated McDonald criteria
topics.coverage_note: v1.3 rebalanced: Bittner 2021 NfL (MS-T1-014) retired (overlapped extensively with Khalil 2018 NfL seed). Added GFAP seed (Abdelhak 2022 Nat Rev Neurol, MS-T1-044) to expand beyond NfL-only coverage, and McDonald 2024 biomarker criteria (Montalban 2025 Lancet Neurol, MS-T1-045) as the authoritative integration of serum biomarkers into diagnosis. CHI3L1 and multi-analyte panel literature will surface via R4 (2025 biomarker review anchor).
topics.retired_seed_note: MS-T1-014 (Bittner 2021 Nat Rev Neurol, NfL review) retired to seed-retired-v1.3 in core_seeds.csv. Cross-seed connectivity with Khalil 2018 (MS-T1-013) was high; two NfL seeds produced expansion bias toward NfL literature at the expense of GFAP and CHI3L1 coverage.

topics.why_it_matters: DMT classes; escalation vs treat-to-target; mechanism diversity across anti-CD20, VLA-4, S1P; BTK inhibitors emerging
topics.coverage_note: v1.3 rebalanced: ORATORIO (MS-T1-016) redistributed to TOPIC-08 (progressive MS trial). Seeds now span three mechanisms: OPERA (MS-T1-015, ocrelizumab anti-CD20, RRMS), AFFIRM (MS-T1-042, natalizumab VLA-4 integrin, RRMS), EXPAND (MS-T1-043, siponimod S1P modulation, SPMS). BTK inhibitors (tolebrutinib, fenebrutinib) and alemtuzumab not yet seeded — monitor via T3 velocity filter once Phase 3 data mature.
topics.seed_C_note: MS-T1-043 (Kappos 2018 EXPAND Lancet) added v1.3. Siponimod is the first S1P modulator with an SPMS indication, bridging TOPIC-07 and TOPIC-08. Provides mechanism diversity absent from the B-cell-depletion-only prior seed set.

topics.why_it_matters: PIRA; smoldering inflammation; why relapse suppression ≠ disability prevention; biggest unmet need
topics.seed_C_note: MS-T1-040 (Ontaneda et al. 2016 Lancet) redistributed from retired TOPIC-16; covers progressive disease biology, trial strategy, and repair agendas — fits TOPIC-08 mechanistically.
topics.seed_D_note: MS-T1-016 (Montalban 2017 NEJM, ORATORIO trial) redistributed from TOPIC-07 v1.3. ORATORIO is the pivotal PPMS trial — its citation neighborhood is progressive MS-specific, not RRMS-specific. Progressive MS framing belongs in TOPIC-08 rather than widening TOPIC-07 toward a single drug class.

topics.why_it_matters: EDSS limitations; fatigue; cognition; PRO instruments; trial endpoint gaps; digital health
Depression; anxiety; cognitive impairment; bladder dysfunction; interaction with disability
Relapse-rebound risk; DMT teratogenicity; washout; post-partum; breastfeeding
ADEM differential; neurodevelopmental stakes; IPMSSG criteria; pediatric EBV data
Structural racism; Black/Hispanic disparities; diagnostic delay; access to high-efficacy DMTs
topics.seed_E_note: MS-T1-046 (Okai et al. 2022 Neurology) promoted from T4-017 expert signal to core seed v1.3. Covers DMT access and clinical outcomes disparities for African American patients specifically — fills a gap between epidemiological disparities papers (seeds A-D) and care-access mechanisms.

topics.why_it_matters: Lesion segmentation; progression prediction; treatment recommendation; data-driven disease reclassification; implementation gap; explainability
topics.seed_C_note: MS-T1-031 (Louapre 2025, AI-driven MS reclassification) redistributed from TOPIC-15 v1.3. AI-based reclassification belongs in the Clinical AI topic; remyelination/neuroprotection is the distinct scientific frontier that TOPIC-15 now anchors exclusively.

topics.why_it_matters: Remyelination biology; neuroprotection strategies; repair beyond inflammation suppression; EBV vaccine prevention; biggest scientific frontier
topics.coverage_note: v1.3 renamed from 'Emerging Frontiers' to 'Remyelination & Neuroprotection' for clean scope. AI reclassification (Louapre 2025, MS-T1-031) redistributed to TOPIC-14 where it belongs mechanistically. Lubetzki 2020 Lancet Neurol review (MS-T1-049) added as second seed to anchor remyelination-to-clinical-translation pipeline. Franklin 2008 (MS-T1-032) remains as foundational remyelination biology anchor.

topics.why_it_matters: Exercise as therapy; multidisciplinary rehabilitation; fatigue management; bladder/bowel; non-pharmacological care; implementation into MS programs
topics.seed_A_note: MS-T1-047 (Dalgas et al. 2019 JAMA Neurol, 'Exercise as medicine in MS') promoted from T4-035 expert signal v1.3. High-impact editorial framing exercise as disease-modifying therapy; JAMA Neurol positioning signals field-level importance shift.
topics.seed_B_note: MS-T1-048 (Khan & Amatya 2017 Arch Phys Med Rehabil) promoted from T4-039 expert signal v1.3. Umbrella review of systematic reviews on MS rehabilitation; most comprehensive evidence synthesis for multidisciplinary care.
topics.rationale: TOPIC-16 added v1.3 to accommodate symptom management, rehabilitation, and multidisciplinary care literature that was previously floating across TOPIC-09 (PROs) and TOPIC-10 (Comorbidities) without a dedicated anchor. This literature addresses JLA Priority #3 (quality of life and symptom management) and is structurally distinct from PRO measurement instruments (TOPIC-09) and comorbidity epidemiology (TOPIC-10). T4 entries T4-035 through T4-041 (rehabilitation/exercise) naturally anchor this topic.

retired_topics.reason: Research Priorities & Cures Roadmap content is better served as expansion fuel from framing seeds than as a standalone topic. Roadmap papers (MS-T1-033, MS-T1-034) promoted to framing seeds R7/R8 to unlock reference-list expansion algorithmically. Ontaneda 2016 (MS-T1-040) redistributed to TOPIC-08 where it fits mechanistically. SDM T4 entries (T4-047 to T4-052) re-mapped to TOPIC-09.

review_anchors.title: Multiple sclerosis
review_anchors.authors: Reich DS, Lucchinetti CF, Calabresi PA

review_anchors.title: MS progression: new mechanism-driven framework
review_anchors.authors: Kuhlmann T, Moccia M, Cohen JA, Mowry EM et al.

review_anchors.title: Blood and CSF biomarkers for MS: emerging clinical applications
Health disparities and inequities in MS and related disorders
Diagnosis of MS: 2024 revisions of McDonald criteria
review_anchors.authors: Montalban X, Oh J, Banwell B et al.

review_anchors.title: Pathways to cures for MS: research roadmap 2022
Refined Pathways to Cures Research Roadmap 2024
